message:
chore(beads): add cross-agent review issue and close audit issue

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- Add new issue bd-pjxw1 "Cross-agent code review and defect
  remediation" (closed): tracked the audit that found and fixed
  defects across web and installer code written by other agents
- Close issue bd-q1igd "Random code-path audit and obvious-bug
  remediation": marked as completed after tracing installer/web/
  manifest flows and verifying doctor checks preserve manifest
  run_as context

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-pdxz","title":"[Phase 8] DCG Website Lessons System Integration","description":"## Phase 8: Website Lessons System Integration

Create a dedicated DCG lesson and integrate DCG into the existing safety-tools lesson.

### Background

The ACFS learning hub has lessons for each major tool (NTM, UBS, CASS, etc.). Each lesson has:
- A React component (apps/web/components/lessons/X-lesson.tsx)
- An entry in lessons.ts (defines slug, title, description, duration)
- Registration in the index.tsx switch statement

Currently, DCG has no dedicated lesson, and the safety-tools-lesson only covers SLB and CAAM.

### Why This Matters

Lessons are how users learn to use tools effectively. Without a DCG lesson, users won't understand:
- What DCG protects against
- How to test commands before running
- How to handle blocked commands
- How to configure packs

### Implementation Details

1. Create dcg-lesson.tsx component
2. Add DCG lesson entry to lessons.ts
3. Register DCG in lessons/index.tsx
4. Update safety-tools-lesson.tsx to include DCG

### Files to Create

- apps/web/components/lessons/dcg-lesson.tsx

### Files to Modify

- apps/web/lib/lessons.ts: Add DCG lesson entry
- apps/web/components/lessons/index.tsx: Register DCG lesson
- apps/web/components/lessons/safety-tools-lesson.tsx: Add DCG section

### Acceptance Criteria

- /learn/dcg route works and shows DCG lesson
- Lesson appears in learning hub navigation
- Safety tools lesson mentions DCG alongside SLB/CAAM","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:49:49.173506194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:21:51.803171831Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:21:51.803171831Z","close_reason":"Added dcg-lesson component, wired slug in lessons index, and expanded safety-tools lesson to include DCG","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pdxz","depends_on_id":"bd-7ifk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:52:40.400624971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-pjxw1","title":"Cross-agent code review and defect remediation","description":"Review code recently written or heavily revised by other agents across the repo, trace adjacent execution flows, identify real bugs/regressions/security or reliability issues, and fix them with verification.

- Sample multiple actively-edited areas rather than a single file.
- Distinguish true defects from mere style differences.
- Apply fixes with the smallest justified diffs and run relevant verification.

 {"id":"bd-pkvn","title":"[Phase A] Core Flywheel Website Integration","description":"# Phase A: Core Flywheel Website Integration

## Purpose
This is the highest-impact phase. The flywheel.ts file is the heart of the /flywheel page visualization. Adding RU here makes it visible to all users browsing the tools.

## Scope
All changes in apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts:
1. Add RU to flywheelTools array
2. Add RU synergy explanations
3. Add RU workflow scenarios
4. Update agent prompts to include RU in bestWith arrays

## Files Modified
- apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts (single file, ~700 lines)

## Technical Details

### Tool Entry Structure
The FlywheelTool type requires:
- id: 'ru'
- name: 'Repo Updater'
- shortName: 'RU'
- href: GitHub URL
- icon: Lucide icon name (suggest 'GitMerge' or 'RefreshCw')
- color: Tailwind gradient (suggest 'from-indigo-400 to-blue-500')
- tagline: Short phrase
- description: 1-2 sentences
- deepDescription: Detailed explanation
- connectsTo: Array of tool IDs RU integrates with
- connectionDescriptions: Object explaining each connection
- stars: GitHub star count
- features: Array of feature strings
- cliCommands: Array of example commands
- installCommand: curl install command
- language: 'Bash'

### Connections
RU connects to:
- ntm: Uses robot mode for agent-sweep and review
- mail: Coordinates repo claims across agents
- bv: Tracks tasks across multiple repos

## Why This Matters
The flywheel page is the visual centerpiece showing how tools work together. Without RU here, users don't see it as part of the ecosystem.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RU appears in flywheel visualization with correct connections
- [ ] Hovering RU shows synergy with ntm, mail, bv
- [ ] New workflow scenario visible in scenarios section
- [ ] Agent prompts updated with ru in bestWith","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:59:53.108966058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T04:22:46.519166247Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T04:22:46.519166247Z","close_reason":"All Phase A tasks complete: flywheel.ts, synergies, workflows, agent prompts, commands.ts, workflow page","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pkvn","depends_on_id":"bd-xa2o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:05:31.763838219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-plgo","title":"apr: Add checksum to checksums.yaml","description":"# Add apr Checksum to checksums.yaml

## Context
ACFS requires SHA256 checksum verification for all curl-installed tools.

## Technical Details
**Location**: `checksums.yaml`

## Steps
1. Download: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/apr/main/install.sh`
2. Compute: `sha256sum install.sh`
3. Add to checksums.yaml

## Entry Structure
```yaml
apr:
  install.sh:
    sha256: <computed-hash>
    url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/apr/main/install.sh
    verified_at: 2026-01-15
    verified_by: manual
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checksum computed and verified
- [ ] Entry added to checksums.yaml
- [ ] Verification succeeds during install","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T18:20:46.978420356Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T18:51:43.802464713Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T18:51:43.802464713Z","close_reason":"Added apr checksum","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["apr","checksums","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-plgo","depends_on_id":"bd-03hr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T18:38:32.581820510Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-pu11","title":"Fix CAAM 'list' to 'ls' in safety-tools-lesson.tsx","description":"BACKGROUND
==========
During deep-dive analysis of the CAAM source code and README, discovered that the safety-tools-lesson.tsx uses 'caam list' but the correct command is 'caam ls'.

SOURCE VERIFICATION
===================
From /tmp/dicklesworthstone_tools/caam/README.md line 210:
  | caam ls [tool] | List all saved profiles in vault |

From CAAM CLI source code (no 'list' subcommand defined - 'ls' is the command).

The README Quick Start section (line 193-196) also shows:
  $ caam ls claude
  alice@gmail.com
  bob@gmail.com
  carol@gmail.com

ISSUE LOCATIONS
===============
1. Line 229: Command list shows 'caam list [tool]'
   Should be: 'caam ls [tool]'

2. Line 344: Quick Reference shows 'caam list [tool]'
   Should be: 'caam ls [tool]'

FIX
===
Replace 'caam list' with 'caam ls' in both locations.

REASONING
=========
- 'ls' is the canonical command per CAAM README and source code
- 'list' is NOT a valid CAAM command (will error if user tries it)
- Lesson content must match official tool documentation to avoid confusion

TESTING
=======
After fix, verify commands match CAAM README section 'Command Reference > Auth File Swapping'","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T23:00:17.385730Z","updated_at":"2025-12-25T23:01:00.475866Z","closed_at":"2025-12-25T23:01:00.475866Z","close_reason":"Fixed 'caam list' to 'caam ls' in both command list (line 229) and quick reference (line 345)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pu11","depends_on_id":"bd-lyf0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-25T23:00:24.093155Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-q7r3","title":"[EPIC] First SSH Connection Experience","description":"# Epic: First SSH Connection Experience

## Problem Statement
The first SSH connection is where the most beginners fail and abandon the process. Three critical issues:

1. **"Type yes" terror**: The host authenticity warning looks like a SECURITY ALERT. Users are afraid to type "yes" because it looks like they're accepting malware.

2. **Password confusion**: Users confuse their VPS provider account password (Hetzner login) with the VPS root password (set during VPS creation). These are DIFFERENT passwords.

3. **Post-connection disorientation**: User successfully connects, sees "root@vps:~#", has no idea what this means or that they're now "inside" the VPS.

## Background & Reasoning
SSH is a completely foreign concept. "Secure Shell" means nothing to beginners. The mental model of "remote controlling another computer through text" is not intuitive.

The host authenticity prompt is particularly problematic because it uses security language ("ECDSA key fingerprint", "Are you sure you want to continue?") that sounds dangerous. Security-conscious users will hesitate; naive users will learn bad habits.

The password confusion is a classic support issue. Users try their Hetzner account password 3 times, get locked out, and panic.

## User Story
As a beginner making my first SSH connection,
I want clear guidance through each prompt and potential error,
So that I can successfully connect without fear or confusion.

## Success Criteria
1. User understands the "type yes" prompt is normal and safe
2. User knows which password to use (VPS root, not provider account)
3. User understands they're now "inside" the VPS after connecting
4. User can verify they're on the VPS (e.g., run hostname)
5. User knows how to disconnect (exit)

## Scope
- Enhance ssh-connect wizard page with more detailed guidance
- Add prominent password clarification
- Add post-connection verification step
- Improve troubleshooting for common errors
- Add mental model explanation for "being inside" the VPS

## Dependencies
- Epic A: Terminal Onboarding Foundation (user must understand terminal)
- Epic B: SSH Key Generation (user must have generated key)

## UI/UX Requirements
- Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile
- Password clarification should be PROMINENT, not buried in SimplerGuide
- Use AlertCard for critical warnings (which password to use)
- Consider showing the scary prompt so users recognize it

## Technical Approach
1. Add AlertCard above the SSH command explaining password distinction
2. Add OutputPreview showing the "type yes" prompt with explanation
3. Add post-connection verification: "Try running: hostname"
4. Enhance troubleshooting with password-specific guidance
5. Add "You're now inside the VPS" confirmation section

## Estimated Effort
Medium (1 wizard page major enhancement)

## Priority Justification
P0 (Critical) - This is the highest drop-off point in the wizard. Users who fail here never complete setup.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T18:34:56.174103Z","updated_at":"2025-12-22T19:48:02.756085Z","closed_at":"2025-12-22T19:48:02.756085Z","close_reason":"Implemented: Added prominent password distinction warning (VPS root vs provider account), 'type yes' prompt preview with explanation, password prompt with invisible typing note. All success criteria met.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q7r3","depends_on_id":"bd-1wk6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T18:37:49.119933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-q7r3","depends_on_id":"bd-ux1f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T18:37:43.878369Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
